patient_summary: Olivia, female, age 73, DOB 05/05/1952 (conflicting DOB 07/21/1954 present in one source). Major diagnoses documented include mixed dyslipidemia, essential hypertension, postoperative/unspecified anemia, leg pain, and hyperlipidemia associated with type 2 diabetes mellitus. Key recent labs (07/03/2023) show Ferritin 266.8 (H) ng/mL, Iron 41 (L) ug/dL with Iron Saturation 15% (L), eGFR CKD-EPI 58 (L) ml/min/1.73m2, TSH 1.320 mIU/mL, triglycerides 158 (H) mg/dL and LDL 62 mg/dL. Historic labs include elevated glucose 199 mg/dL (01/11/2021) and renal markers with creatinine 1.1 (H) and GFR 50 (01/11/2021). Ongoing care involves primary care (Hand, Hoyet A, MD; Hershey, Curtis L, MD), cardiology, endocrinology, and oncology specialists. Multiple upcoming encounters and a planned ancillary procedure are documented. Social history notes former cigarette use and no current alcohol. Functional assessments document difficulty walking; dressing/bathing responses vary by date. Conflicting demographic data and all source references preserved.
